rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Training creator's name banned in student-facing body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'Antti', 'the creator', 'the author', 'course creator' in body (lines 1-72, above maintainer cut).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Author-"we" ban — first-person-plural extension.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No author-we usage. Line 26 'you' correctly refers to student (You walk away / come back). Line 31-32 uses 'the agent' not author voice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No reassurance phrasings like 'it's OK if', 'no penalty', 'fine way to', 'not a downgrade'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (lines 3-4) is punchy and holds up. No overstatement that needs carve-out. Key Concepts land sharp without hedging.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body teaches the loop, infrastructure, and walk-away autonomy without defensive positioning against vendors or consultancy. No 'real practitioners' framing, no 'unlike vendor' language.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 50 'Ask Claude' (peer, not combative). Line 26 'the system keeps absorbing' (agent as collaborator, not subordinate). No 'command', 'force', 'drag', 'bark' at agent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea frames forward (work tightens / generator sharpens). Key Concepts name disciplines (walk-away autonomy, role separation) not student deficits.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'Judge' is earned in context (Module 5's judge becomes infrastructure, lines 3-4, 24, 39). 'Eval loop' earned through the Big Idea narrative. 'Tactic' appears at line 18, earned in Module 5 reference. No OOB cold terms.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default).
rules_evaluated.evidence: No unearned tech words flagged for Bootstrap SVP-audience. 'Infrastructure' is body-concrete (line 18), not jargon. 'Tactic' earned (line 25). Language is peer-direct throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No confusion. File does not engage memory vs. context distinction at this level — not a loaded surface for this module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in the body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body reads as calm forward narrative (Big Idea, 'Start here', Prework, Lectures, Exercises). Troubleshooting and maintenance notes below `<!-- maintainer -->` (lines 74+).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 4 'You walk away / you come back' — correct second-person. Line 26 'What if you weren't in the room' — correct address. No 'the student' in body. Trainer framing below divider (lines 74+).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'Groundedness-judge' is earned at line 24 (reference to Module 5 winner). 'Role separation' earned in line 40 conceptual section. No maintainer-only terms paraphrased into body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No narrating the student's past or present state
rules_evaluated.evidence: No patronizing recap of work done. Line 25 'Module 5 ended with a winner' describes the prior artifact, not the student's state. No 'You've done the work' pep-talk prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No em-dashes found. Sentences are simple or compound with conjunctions. Lines read cleanly aloud.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'Start here', 'Lectures', 'Exercises', 'Key Concepts', 'Debrief', 'Next' — canonical shape per curriculum/CLAUDE.md. Exempt from verb-header rule. Internal structure complies (e.g., 'Debrief' subsection naming, not 'What to do in the Debrief').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 28 '[Lecture: Evals as steering](lectures/evals-as-steering.md)' — title-based link. Line 34-36 all use title labels. No `reference/...` path in link text.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 26 'while you step away' / 'when you come back' use session-relative phrasing, not time-of-day. No 'tomorrow', 'overnight', 'this morning', 'next morning'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'Pause', 'Meta-moment', 'Step back', 'Reflection beat', 'take a moment to' in body. Debrief section exists and names the move as a structural element (line 45: 'Debrief'), not as introspective theatre. Line 49 prompt names the work directly, no theater wrapper.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt at lines 52-59 contains no `[BRACKETS]` placeholders. Content references 'this session', 'module-6/runs/', 'judges/groundedness-judge.md' — concrete paths and scrollback references, not fill-in slots.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 51 'Ask Claude to read the round trail and sharpen the generator's tactic beyond what the loop reached.' — one sentence, command verb, semantic summary under 20 words.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 50-52: lead-in, `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` label, blank line, fenced block starting line 53. No wrapping blockquotes. No `copy →` prefix. No `(end of prompt)` closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt does not invoke skills by path or name. No skill invocation in fenced block. N/A for this prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, never by invented alias.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt references './generation-tactic.md', 'module-6/runs/', 'module-6/eval-notes.md', 'judges/groundedness-judge.md' — all deterministic paths (line 53-58). No fuzzy fallbacks or invented aliases. Anchors are scrollback references ('the integrity of the loop').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-sample methods need structural enforcement in the prompt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt does not use multi-sample detection or ensemble method. Single-run eval-loop with fixed judge. N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout (`**bold**`, `*italic*`) inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight in Claude Code.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 53-59 use backticks for paths (./generation-tactic.md, module-6/) and bullet formatting for clarity. No bold/italic markdown inside fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Citation discipline — extend cite-or-flag scope, and quote the supporting sentence.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt does not make factual claims requiring citation. Operates on locally-supplied artifacts (session history, judge file, tactic file). N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Niceness tax — name the edge or pin the line on regeneration.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt is a one-time ask, not a regeneration. No niceness drift. Direct, sparse language. 'integrate, don't append' (line 56) names the edge clearly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Self-critique register, not defense, when Claude interrogates its own work.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 51 'interrogate' language is accurate (not 'defend'). Line 54-55 'which tactic change helped, which did not help' — critique register, not defense.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Tell me what you wrote, not show me what you'll write.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 58-59: 'tell me in 3-5 lines... When you're done, tell me...' — post-action report, not pre-confirm. Force integration accountability at artifact level.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (lines 3-4) names the mood of unleashed leverage — work tightens under fixed judge, student walks away and returns to sharpened system. 'Walk-away autonomy' is the emotional territory. Positioned as setup for the closing integration (Module 8 awe/discovery).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea is epistemic-not-punitive. Student learns what's possible (autonomy, compounded improvement) through loop experience, not through failure shame. 'Walk away, come back to a sharper generator' is discovery-mood, not corrective.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea leads with 'grounded' loop (fixed judge, integrity, tightening work). Key Concepts name positive disciplines: 'Eval as infrastructure', 'Role separation', 'Walk-away autonomy', 'The work gets sharper because the judge stays still.' Failure modes are negative cases, not titles.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module sequence respects curriculum strategy. Prework anchors to Mollick's Bitter Lesson (line 8, 20-22) — no forward-run of Module 8 existential themes. Lecture + Exercise + Closing embed the eval loop teaching cleanly. No skill front-run.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts (lines 38-43) surface Bootstrap recurring themes: compounding infrastructure (eval as machinery), role clarity (generation/judging separation), learner agency (walk-away autonomy, the student observes impact). No isolated tactics.
